title: Hieracium mitkae
taxonomy: Hieracium mitkae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, commonly known as hawkweeds. The species is further grouped into the subgenus Hieracium and the section Hieracium. Within the Asteraceae family, it is most closely related to other dandelion-like plants. Further classification details may require more specific botanical information.

intro: Hieracium mitkae, also known as Mitka's hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Hieracium mitkae has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium mitkae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Hieracium mitkae is often combined with other plants to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
